description:
Add crates/balls-plugin-github-issues/README.md describing the
issues plugin and its bidirectional behavior. Update the workspace's
top-level README to point at both per-plugin READMEs. Document the
config schema, the close-mirror authority decision, the auto-create
policy, and the live-test recipe.

WHY: future operators (and agents) need to understand the authority
model before they enable the plugin. "GH-issue closed automatically
closes the balls task" is a real surprise if the docs don't say so
up front. Bake the why into the docs, not just the commit message,
per balls's "philosophy in long-lived artifacts" convention.

ACCEPTANCE:
- crates/balls-plugin-github-issues/README.md exists and covers:
  what it does, how to configure, the close-mirror authority + how
  to disable it, the auto-create policy + the label-gated mode if
  configured, the separate-token-from-forge auth model with the
  rotate-independently rationale, and the live-sandbox test recipe.
- Top-level README has a "Plugins in this workspace" section linking
  to each per-plugin README.
- A "Don't merge these into one participant" subsection (one
  paragraph) for future agents, citing
  SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants §3/§5.
- Both READMEs are under 300 lines (md files are gate-exempt but
  staying under is good form).
title: README for balls-plugin-github-issues + workspace docs cleanup
